sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Everton have struggled recently, with no wins in their last 4 matches, managing only 2 draws and conceding in 3 of those games. Crystal Palace, on the other hand, are in strong form with 4 wins in their last 5 matches and an unbeaten streak of 11 games, showing attacking prowess and defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Everton have had the upper hand, winning both encounters with scores of 2-1 and 2-1. This suggests Everton have a slight edge at home against Crystal Palace, often finding ways to score late.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Everton have a balanced record, scoring 7 goals and conceding 6 this season, with strong defensive stats (5th least goals conceded). Crystal Palace, ranked 5th, have scored 8 and conceded only 3, showcasing a robust defense and effective late-game scoring (33% of goals in the 76-90 minute range).

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Everton’s home form and historical advantage over Crystal Palace could play a role, but their current winless streak raises concerns. Palace’s consistent form and ability to score in crucial moments make them slight favorites despite being away.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Crystal Palace lead Everton by a score of 0-1 at Everton Stadium. The sole goal came from Daniel Muñoz in the 37th minute, securing a narrow advantage for the visitors. Crystal Palace have shown dominance in key areas, while Everton have struggled to convert their limited opportunities into goals. The home side will need a strong response in the second half to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Crystal Palace have been the more commanding side in the first half, enjoying greater ball possession and creating more threatening moments in the final third. Their ability to win duels, particularly in the air, has given them an edge over Everton. The visitors have also been more clinical in their approach, capitalizing on a key chance to take the lead. Everton, on the other hand, have shown resilience defensively but lack sharpness in attack. Despite creating a big chance, they failed to convert, which has left them trailing at the break. Their entries into the penalty area have been limited, and they will need to push harder to break through Palace's defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: Crystal Palace started the game with a strong attacking presence, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages. Their momentum continued with several spells of dominance, particularly in the middle of the half, where they pushed forward with intent. The visitors had a decisive attacking surge leading up to their goal, showcasing their ability to capitalize on momentum shifts. Everton had brief moments of attacking intent, with short bursts in the first half, but struggled to sustain pressure. Their momentum faded as Palace regained control, leaving the home side chasing the game at the interval.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players and standouts
halftimeSections.text: For Crystal Palace, Daniel Muñoz has been the standout performer, not only scoring the crucial goal but also contributing defensively. His presence in both attack and defense has been vital for the away side. Ismaïla Sarr also played a significant role with an assist, showing creativity in the final third. On Everton's side, James Garner has been influential in midfield, creating a big chance and linking play effectively. Goalkeeper Jordan Pickford has also been a key figure, making important interventions to keep the scoreline close despite the pressure from Palace.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Everton are expected to come out with renewed energy in the second half, driven by the need to level the scoreline in front of their home crowd. They will likely focus on increasing their presence in the final third and capitalizing on any set-piece opportunities. Crystal Palace, meanwhile, will aim to maintain their control and exploit counter-attacking chances to extend their lead. Their defensive organization will be tested as Everton push for an equalizer, but Palace have shown the ability to handle pressure so far. The second half could see a more open game if Everton commit players forward, potentially leaving spaces for Palace to exploit.
